sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचपश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहेसर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान् । ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थमृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान् ॥
hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले  हे देव  मैं आपके शरीरमें सम्पूर्ण देवताओंको, प्राणियोंके विशेषविशेष समुदायोंको कमलासनपर बैठे हुए ब्रह्माजीको, शङ्करजीको, सम्पूर्ण ऋषियोंको और सम्पूर्ण दिव्य सर्पोंको देख रहा हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna said: O divine one, I see all the gods in your body, and all kinds of distinct beings, Brahma seated on the lotus, Shiva, all the sages, and all the radiant serpents.
essence: The many forms of existence appear within one vast body.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna sees the entire divine order inside Krishna's cosmic body: gods, beings of every kind, Brahma, Shiva, sages, and radiant serpents.
simpleInsight: A single vision can collapse the boundary between the many and the one.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna has already seen Krishna's cosmic form and is overwhelmed. He now describes what that vision contains: every class of being, from gods to sages to Brahma and Shiva, all within Krishna's body. The next verse expands the awe even further.
modernRelevance: You stare at a decision that seems private, then suddenly see how many lives, histories, and forces are braided into it. The choice is bigger than your fear.
translationLiteral: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / पश्यामि (I see) / देवान् (the gods) / तव (your) / देव (O divine one) / देहे (in the body) / सर्वान् (all) / तथा (and) / भूतविशेषसङ्घान् (groups of distinct beings) / ब्रह्माणम् (Brahma) / ईशम् (the lord) / कमलासनस्थम् (seated on the lotus seat) / ऋषीन् (sages) / च (and) / सर्वान् (all) / उरगान् (serpents) / च (and) / दिव्यान् (radiant / divine)
insight: It shifts perception from isolated details to the whole living field contained in one reality.
